User Input: How do I view job details?

User Input: What information is shown on a job listing?
Response: Each job listing shows the title category country city posting date salary and full description. Employer accounts will not see the Apply button.

User Input: How do I apply for a job?
Response: Open the job listing you want and click the Apply Now button. Fill in your name email phone number address cover letter and upload your resume then click Send Application.

User Input: How to submit a job application?
Response: Open the job listing and click Apply Now. Fill in the application form with your details and resume then submit.
Open the job listing you want and click Apply Now. Complete the application form with your personal details cover letter and resume upload.

User Input: Steps to apply for a job

User Input: What do I need to apply for a job?
Response: You need to fill in your full name email phone number address and cover letter and upload your resume in PDF JPG or PNG format.

User Input: What file formats are accepted for resume?
Response: You can upload your resume as a PDF JPG or PNG file when applying for a job.
Job Dekho accepts resumes in PDF JPG or PNG format. Make sure your file is one of these types before uploading.

User Input: What format should my resume be in?
Response: Your resume should be in PDF JPG or PNG format.

User Input: Can I apply for multiple jobs?
Response: Yes you can apply to as many jobs as you like by visiting each job listing individually and submitting a separate application for each one.
Yes you can apply for as many jobs as you want. Visit each listing and submit a separate application.

User Input: Can I delete my job application?
Response: You can delete your own pending application from the My Applications page. Once reviewed you cannot delete it.

User Input: How do I withdraw my application?
Response: You can delete your pending application from the My Applications page. If already reviewed contact the employer directly.
Go to My Applications page and click Delete on the pending application you want to cancel. Applications already under review cannot be deleted.

User Input: Where can I see my submitted applications?
Response: Go to MY APPLICATIONS in the sidebar. You will see all your submitted applications with their current status.
Click MY APPLICATIONS in the sidebar to see all your submitted applications along with their current status and timeline.
Open MY APPLICATIONS from the sidebar. Each application shows a status timeline so you can track where it stands.

User Input: How to track my application status?
Response: On the My Applications page each application shows a status timeline with stages Applied Under Review Awaiting Decision Interview Scheduled and Final Decision.

User Input: What are the stages of an application?
Response: The application goes through these stages: Applied then Under Review then Awaiting Decision. If accepted it moves to Interview Scheduled and then Final Decision.

There are five stages: Applied means submitted. Under Review means the employer is looking. Awaiting Decision means nearing a verdict. Interview Scheduled means accepted. Final Decision is the hiring result.
Your application goes to the employer for review. The status shows Under Review on your My Applications page. The employer can then accept or reject it.

User Input: What does it mean if my application is accepted?
Response: The employer has accepted your application and scheduled an interview. You will receive a notification and can see the interview date time and meeting link in My Applications.

User Input: What does it mean if my application is rejected?
Response: The employer has reviewed your application and decided not to move forward. You will see a Rejected status on your application card.
If your application shows Rejected the employer has decided not to proceed. You can continue applying for other jobs on the platform.

User Input: How will I know if I got the interview?
Response: You will receive a real time notification and your application status will update to show the interview date time and meeting link.

User Input: Where do I find my interview details?
Response: Go to MY APPLICATIONS in the sidebar. If accepted you will see the interview date time and a clickable Meet link on your application card.

User Input: How do I join my interview?
Response: Go to My Applications or the Interview Dashboard. Click the Join Meeting link next to your scheduled interview to open the video call.
If your application has been accepted the interview link should appear in your My Applications page under the accepted card. If it is missing contact the employer or support.

The Interview Dashboard shows all accepted applications with scheduled interviews. Job seekers can see their interview schedule and final result. Employers can see all candidates and set the final verdict.

User Input: How to access the Interview Dashboard?
Response: Click on INTERVIEW DASHBOARD in the sidebar navigation menu. It is available to both Job Seekers and Employers.
Click INTERVIEW DASHBOARD in the sidebar to see all your scheduled interviews along with their date time meeting link and final verdict.
After the interview the employer marks each candidate as either Selected for the job or Not Selected. You will see this result in the Interview Dashboard and My Applications.

User Input: How do I know if I got the job?
Response: After your interview the employer will update the final verdict. If selected you will see a Selected badge and receive a notification.

If you see Not Selected it means the employer did not proceed with your application after the interview. Keep applying to other positions available on Job Dekho.

Log in as an Employer then click POST NEW JOB in the sidebar. Fill in all the required fields and click Create Job to publish your listing.

User Input: How to create a job listing?
Response: Log in as an Employer. Go to POST NEW JOB in the sidebar. Fill in the job title category location salary details and description then submit.

User Input: What details are required to post a job?

User Input: What salary types can I choose?
Response: You can choose between a Fixed Salary where you enter one amount or a Ranged Salary where you enter a minimum and maximum salary value.
Fixed Salary means you set one specific pay amount. Ranged Salary means you set a minimum and maximum so candidates see the pay band.

User Input: Can I edit a job I posted?

User Input: How to update a job listing?
Response: Go to VIEW YOUR JOBS click Edit on the job you want to update make your changes and click the checkmark to confirm.
Open VIEW YOUR JOBS from the sidebar click the Edit icon on the relevant job card update the fields you want and click the checkmark to save.

Response: Go to VIEW YOUR JOBS in the sidebar and click the Delete button on the job card you want to remove.
Go to VIEW YOUR JOBS and click the Delete button on the listing you want to remove.

User Input: Can I mark a job as expired?
Response: Yes when editing a job in VIEW YOUR JOBS you can change the Expired field to TRUE to mark it as no longer active.

User Input: How to see the jobs I have posted?
Response: Click on VIEW YOUR JOBS in the sidebar. You will see all your posted jobs with a summary of total active and expired listings.

User Input: Where can I see applications for my jobs?

Click Accept and Schedule Interview on the applicant card in MY APPLICATIONS. Fill in the interview date time and meeting link and confirm.

User Input: How do I reject an application?
Response: Click the Reject Application button on the applicant card in My Applications. The applicant will be notified automatically.

User Input: How do I set the final hiring verdict?

Notifications keep you updated in real time about key events like new applications being submitted jobs posted and application decisions.

User Input: Where can I see my notifications?
Response: Click NOTIFICATIONS in the sidebar. You will see all your notifications with a summary of total unread and read counts.

User Input: How do I mark a notification as read?
Response: Click the green checkmark on any notification card or click Mark All as Read at the top to clear all at once.
Click Mark All as Read at the top of the Notifications page to clear all unread notifications at once.

User Input: What does the notification badge mean?
Response: The badge on the NOTIFICATIONS menu item shows the number of unread notifications. It updates in real time as new events happen.

User Input: What types of notifications are there?
Response: There are four notification types: New Job Posted shown to job seekers Application Submitted shown to employer Application Accepted shown to job seeker and Application Rejected shown to job seeker.
Job Dekho uses Socket.IO so notifications appear instantly without refreshing the page. You will be notified of applications decisions and job postings in real time.

User Input: How do real time notifications work?
Response: The app uses Socket.IO so you receive notifications instantly without needing to refresh. As long as you are logged in updates arrive automatically.

Job Dekho connects job seekers with employers. Seekers search and apply. Employers post jobs review applications accept or reject candidates schedule interviews and record final hiring decisions.

Job seekers register browse jobs and apply. Employers post jobs review applications accept or reject candidates and schedule interviews. The entire hiring process from application to final verdict happens within the platform.

User Input: Tips for writing a cover letter
Response: Keep your cover letter focused on why you are a good fit for the role. Mention relevant skills and experience and keep it professional and concise.

User Input: How to write a good cover letter?
Response: Focus on your relevant skills and why you want this specific role. Be professional concise and tailor it to the job description.
Keep your cover letter brief and specific. Highlight your most relevant experience and explain why you are interested in this particular role.
Keep your resume clear and organised with relevant skills and experience highlighted. Visit our blog section for detailed resume writing guides.
Make sure your resume is clean and well-formatted. List relevant skills work experience and education. Tailor it for each job you apply to.
Use clear sections for skills experience and education. Keep it to one or two pages. Highlight accomplishments and tailor it to each job listing.

User Input: How many jobs can I apply for?
Response: There is no limit. You can apply for as many jobs as you like on Job Dekho.

User Input: Is there a limit on applications?
Response: No there is no limit on how many jobs you can apply for. Apply to as many as you want.

User Input: Can I see who viewed my profile?
Response: Currently Job Dekho does not show profile view counts. Focus on applying directly to job listings.

User Input: How do I check if a job is still open?
Response: If a job listing appears on the All Jobs page it is still active. Jobs marked as Expired by the employer will no longer be visible.
Active jobs appear on the All Jobs page. If you can see it and apply it is still open. Expired jobs are hidden by the employer.

User Input: What happens to my application if a job expires?
Response: If a job is marked as expired after you applied your application is still on record. Contact the employer directly if you have concerns.

User Input: Can the employer contact me directly?
Response: Employers communicate through the platform via the interview scheduling feature. They may also use the contact details in your application if needed.

User Input: How do I know if my application was seen?
Response: Once your application status moves from Applied to Under Review you know the employer has seen it.
Check the status on MY APPLICATIONS. If it still shows Applied wait a bit or contact support at support@jobdekho.com.

User Input: Can I update my application after submitting?
Response: Applications cannot be edited after submission. You may delete a pending application and reapply if changes are needed.
You cannot edit an application after it is submitted. You can delete a pending one and reapply with the corrected information.

Yes Job Dekho is designed to work on both desktop and mobile browsers.

User Input: Does Job Dekho have a mobile app?
Response: Currently Job Dekho is a web application that works in mobile browsers. A dedicated mobile app may be available in the future.
Job Dekho works best on modern browsers like Chrome Firefox Edge and Safari.

User Input: Is my data safe on Job Dekho?
Response: Job Dekho takes data privacy seriously. Your personal information is used only for job matching and application processing.
Your data is used solely for the purpose of job matching and communication within the platform. We do not sell your information.
Your password is encrypted and stored securely. Use a strong unique password and do not share your credentials with anyone.

User Input: How do I get more applicants?
Response: Write a clear and detailed job description with accurate salary information and the right category. Listings with complete information tend to attract more applicants.
Use a clear job title accurate category good description and fair salary to attract quality candidates.

User Input: How do I find tech jobs?
Response: Go to All Jobs and use the search bar to filter by keywords like Developer Engineer or specific technologies. You can also browse categories like Frontend Web Development or MERN Stack.

User Input: Find jobs in a specific city
Response: Use the All Jobs page. While filtering by city is not yet a search bar feature you can browse the city information shown on each job card.

User Input: How do I find remote jobs?

Some job listings may offer remote work. Check the location field and job description for each listing on the All Jobs page.
MERN stands for MongoDB Express React and Node.js. It is a popular full-stack JavaScript technology combination. Job Dekho has a dedicated category for MERN Stack jobs.
MEAN stands for MongoDB Express Angular and Node.js. It is a full-stack JavaScript framework. Job Dekho has a MEAN Stack job category.
MEVN stands for MongoDB Express Vue and Node.js. It is a full-stack JavaScript framework. Job Dekho includes a MEVN Stack job category.

User Input: How do I use this platform?
Response: Job Dekho is simple to use. Register as a Job Seeker or Employer. Job Seekers search and apply for jobs. Employers post jobs and manage applications. Use the sidebar to navigate between all features.
Start by registering as a Job Seeker or Employer on the Register page. Then log in and use the sidebar to explore All Jobs post listings or manage your applications.
